content_body:
If you're coming to Blue Ridge for Spring Break, be sure to add these top spring activities to your North GA bucket list! 

Perhaps one of the most enchanting times of year to experience Blue Ridge, Georgia, is during springtime. Experience quintessential luxury in any of our upscale Blue Ridge cabin rentals featuring beautiful mountain views or river and lake views.

Within 20 minutes of downtown Blue Ridge, an array of experiences await you within your luxury cabin’s surroundings, in town exploring shops, restaurants, and galleries, or outside immersed in the Blue Ridge mountain landscape.

As the days get longer and the temperatures get warmer, the lure of sunshine, fresh mountain air, and colorful flora beckons us to shed the last remains of winter’s chill and embrace the warmth of the season.

Blue Ridge Cabin Rentals: A Springtime Haven

Choosing the right cabin in Blue Ridge can significantly enhance your spring break experience in Blue Ridge, GA. With options ranging from cozy 2-bedrooms with creek access to luxurious lodges overlooking the vast mountain ranges, our luxury cabin rentals offer a tranquil retreat amidst nature's beauty.

Creekside Green

Springtime Activities in Blue Ridge, GA

Explore the quaint and historic streets of Downtown Blue Ridge and experience a variety of shops, restaurants, and attractions. Book a pedaling journey with Blue Ridge Scenic Railway's Railcarts, enjoy a game of mini golf at 2nd Story Entertainment, grab a bite to eat (and a delicious milkshake) at Bob's Eatery, or peruse nostalgic candies, toys, and more at Huck's General Store.

Blue Ridge is particularly family-friendly, and the opportunities are endless for memory-making! Make sure to keep an eye on the area's festivals and event schedule for some extra fun to add to your itinerary.

Blue Ridge Scenic Railway

If you would prefer to view the spring scenery from a slower-pace, experience the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway and enjoy a train ride along the Toccoa River. Taking you from Blue Ridge to nearby McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN. Stand in two states at one time,  dine along the Toccoa River, and shop the unique Riverwalk Shops until you journey back to Blue Ridge. 

Best Spring Hikes in North GA Mountains

Spring is the perfect season for adventure-seekers to hit the trails and discover the beauty that lies just around the bend. Hike the beginning of the Appalachian Trail at Springer Mountain, stroll along the family-friendly Aska Trails System, or visit the Swinging Bridge, a renowned suspension bridge that crosses the Toccoa River on the Benton MacKaye Trail. 

Must-see Waterfall Hikes

Peppered with gorgeous waterfalls, the Blue Ridge area boasts stunning natural falls that are a must for your spring break itinerary. Perhaps the most breathtaking waterfall to explore is the tallest waterfall in Georgia: nearby Amicalola Falls in Ellijay. Spilling 729-feet over boulders and mossy-green rocks, this breathtaking waterfall also boasts long-range mountain views at its peak.

Guided Fly-fishing Trips on the Toccoa River

Blue Ridge is the Trout Capital of Georgia, and for good reason! Our rivers and streams are home to blue ribbon rainbow and brown trout, and the area's fishing guides are enthusiastic about helping you land one of these monsters! Springtime is the perfect time to bring out your rods as fishing is at its prime. Stay in one of our best fishing cabins in Blue Ridge and cast your line a few steps away!

Mercier Orchards

Visit the largest and oldest orchard in the South, Mercier Orchards, located just 7 minutes from downtown Blue Ridge. A family-owned farm since 1943, Mercier Orchards provides U-Pick events, an award-winning bakery, a public event venue, home decor, a hard cider tasting room, and so much more. No visit to Mercier's is complete without a dozen fried apple pies to take home with you! Pro tip: heat them up in the microwave for 7ish seconds and top with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

Horseback Riding Through the Mountains

Many horseback riding outfitters dot the North GA mountain canvas and provide memorable trail rides through the rolling hills and valleys. Channel your inner cowboy or cowgirl and fall in love with the surrounding beauty as the mountains wake up from their winter slumber.

Tee Time in the North GA Mountains

Several golf courses drape the landscape of these North Georgia Mountains. Enjoy a round of golf at Old Toccoa Farms, where rolling fairways and greens front over 4,000 feet of the Toccoa River. Brasstown Valley Golf Course, another popular course ranked by Golf Digest as one of the best golf courses in Georgia, is just 45 minutes away.

Family-Friendly Activities

Blue Ridge is a destination for all ages, with plenty of family-friendly activities to enjoy together. Whether it's exploring the great outdoors or enjoying the town's attractions, there's something for everyone in the family to enjoy.

Book Your Spring Getaway

Experience a memorable spring break in Blue Ridge, GA with our guide to the best activities and enjoy the lap of luxury at one of our cabin rentals. From riverfront to mountain view and 2-bedrooms to 5-bedrooms, our properties offer the perfect opportunity to experience the North Georgia Mountains this spring. Book online or over the phone at (706) 432-2140 today!
title: Spring Things To Do in Blue Ridge, GA
